title: Release Report
description: release/release-report.json. Produced by the Reporter Agent from context.json + test-cases + failure-analysis + bug drafts. coverage_by_risk is derived from the traceability chain — see docs/traceability.md.

summary.description: Short prose summary of the run.

coverage_by_risk.description: One entry per RISK-XXX from context.json.risks[]. Derived from the traceability chain.

status.description: Coverage status for this risk in this run.

execution_summary.description: Phase 1 form is a single flat object. Phase 1.5 (TG3) switches to a grouped {e2e, api, combined} form. Both forms are accepted via oneOf.

uncovered_risks.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 2.6, Improvement 3). risk_ids from context.json.risks[] that have ZERO covering test cases — i.e. coverage_by_risk entries whose status is 'uncovered'. Derived deterministically from the traceability chain; no LLM. Surfaced explicitly so a reviewer sees gaps without having to scan coverage_by_risk by hand.

uncovered_high_severity_count.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 2.6). Count of uncovered_risks whose severity in context.json.risks[] is 'high'. When > 0 the release_recommendation should not be 'pass' (a high-severity risk with no test is a coverage gap).

summary_by_risk_level.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). Per-severity rollup of coverage_by_risk, so a reviewer reads the release at a glance: how many high/medium/low risks are passing, failing, or uncovered. Derived deterministically from coverage_by_risk + context.json.risks[].severity; no LLM.

untested_high_risk_items.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). The high-severity risks with NO covering test (the named version of uncovered_high_severity_count). 'No hidden untested risks' — the report must surface these, never omit them.

flaky_tests.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). Tests that passed on retry / showed instability, IF flakiness is tracked for this run. Empty or omitted when not tracked — never fabricated.

open_bugs_summary.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). Rollup of bug_drafts[] for the release decision: how many open bugs, by severity, and how many have / lack a Jira key.

conditional_pass_criteria.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). When release_recommendation is 'conditional_pass', the explicit, checkable conditions that must hold before release — the structured form of what release_recommendation_reasoning states in prose. The rule 'conditional_pass MUST list its conditions' is satisfied by either; this field makes them machine-checkable.

external_links.description: OPTIONAL (Phase 3 TG12). Links to external systems for this release: TestLink test plan, Jira issues/epics, dashboards. Tool-agnostic { system, label, url } so the project stays generic (TestLink + Jira today, others later). URLs only — never embed credentials.

blocking_failures.description: FAIL-XXX ids whose presence flips the release recommendation to fail/conditional_pass/blocked.

release_recommendation_reasoning.description: Required free-text. conditional_pass MUST list the conditions explicitly.

severity.description: Mirrors failure.severity from analysis/failure-analysis.json. Only red failures produce bug drafts; yellow is documented here when it was promoted to a draft for human triage.

jira_key_if_exists.description: Populated by Phase 2 scripts/create-jira-bugs.js --apply. Empty/omitted in Phase 1.

evidence_paths.description: Relative paths to evidence the reviewer might want to open: HTML report, traces directory, key screenshots. Reporter must reference paths, never inline contents.

open_questions.description: Things the human needs to answer before the report can move to status='finalized'.

riskLevelRollup.description: Counts of risks at one severity level, by coverage status. total = covered_passing + covered_failing + covered_partial + accepted_without_test + uncovered.
